shipTransit.darkFleetNote: Many vessels transit with AIS disabled. Low AIS count confirms elevated risk — when operators feel safe enough to keep transponders on, that’s the normalcy signal.
shipTransit.source: WTO Hormuz Trade Tracker / AIS Data

events.event: Trump deadline for Iran to reopen Strait (extended to Apr 6)
events.impact: Military escalation threatened if Strait not reopened

events.event: US SPR 400M barrel release runs dry
events.impact: Emergency buffer exhausted — no more cushion

events.event: US waiver for India buying Russian crude expires

events.impact: If not open by mid-April — uncharted territory

straitStatus.description: Iran maintains selective control through controlled transit regime. Limited passage for approved nations (China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan, Malaysia, Thailand); blockade of US, Israel, and allied vessels. Approximately 90% of normal traffic blocked.

alternativeRoutes.description: Significantly increased rerouting activity; capacity constraints emerging at East African ports.
Pipeline capacity maxed; unable to compensate for maritime closure; limited additional throughput.
Constrained by Red Sea security concerns and Houthi threats; increased insurance costs.

regionalImpact.description: Imports 40% of oil and 30% of LNG via Hormuz. 55 Chinese-flagged ships trapped in Persian Gulf.
Heavily dependent on Middle East oil via Hormuz; supply disruption threatens industrial production.
Import-dependent nation; oil import costs surging, affecting refiner margins and energy sector.
12-14% of LNG sourced from Qatar via Hormuz; alternative sourcing expensive and limited.
Permitted limited vessel transits through negotiation; still facing supply constraints and higher costs.

crisisTimeline.title: Status quo: controlled transit, ongoing negotiations
crisisTimeline.description: Strait under selective Iranian control with ~6% of normal traffic. Trump extended deadline to April 6. Estimated 2000 ships stranded. War risk insurance still 6-7x normal.

crisisTimeline.title: China-Pakistan present 5-point ceasefire initiative
crisisTimeline.description: China and Pakistan jointly present peace proposal including immediate ceasefire, halt to infrastructure attacks, safe passage through Strait, and framework for comprehensive peace.

crisisTimeline.title: Iran selectively allows approved nations to transit
crisisTimeline.description: Iran permits passage for vessels from China, Russia, India, Iraq, Pakistan. Malaysia and Thailand granted access following direct talks. UN humanitarian and fertilizer shipments permitted.

crisisTimeline.title: Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum to open Strait
crisisTimeline.description: Trump warns Iran that power plants will be obliterated unless Strait fully opens within 48 hours. Iran responds with threats to target US/Israeli energy infrastructure and lay sea mines.

crisisTimeline.title: US releases 400M barrels from Strategic Petroleum Reserve
crisisTimeline.description: Largest SPR release in history. Estimated 95 days of supply at current drawdown rate. Global coordinated release with IEA members.

crisisTimeline.title: US Navy deploys carrier strike group to Gulf of Oman
crisisTimeline.description: USS Eisenhower carrier strike group positioned in Gulf of Oman. Additional destroyer deployments to escort allied vessels. Iran IRGC navy conducts counter-exercises.

crisisTimeline.title: Iran restricts Strait of Hormuz transit
crisisTimeline.description: Iran begins controlled transit regime following escalation of US-Iran tensions over nuclear program. Initial blockade affects all non-allied vessels. Oil prices spike 15% overnight.
